element_introduction_order.mastery_context: knife enables instant close-range kills; handgun primary ranged option throughout
required to access new areas; collected during exploration
health recovery; occasionally dropped by defeated guards
dropped by defeated guards; replenish ammunition for all firearms
used for destroying obstacles and environmental hazards; limited carrying capacity tied to rank
required for underwater progression in side-view areas; auto-depletes oxygen gauge
applied to officers for interrogation; counts toward rank promotion alongside hostage rescues
communications with three fixed contacts; cannot be used during alert phase
activates when detecting nearby tracking signals; aids navigation and enemy awareness
rescuing scattered hostages triggers rank promotion; necessary for accessing higher-tier equipment
triggered by guard detection; requires defeating soldiers to return to infiltration or escape to adjacent screen
story-critical confrontations; impostor John, Big Boss as final boss

typical_relief_triggers: returning to infiltration phase after alert
obtaining new equipment or card key
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: episodic infiltration with escalating tension; varied environmental challenges (jungle → warehouse → cargo ship → train → main base) prevent rhythm monotony; side-view sections create mechanical breaks from top-down stealth

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: side-view areas function as mandatory progression gates rather than optional exploration; varied biomes (jungle, warehouse, cargo ship, train) signal new mission phases

difficulty_curve.early_game: introduction to infiltration mechanics and basic guard avoidance in jungle setting; player begins with knife and handgun for immediate defensive capability
difficulty_curve.mid_game: escalation through multiple facility types (warehouse, cargo ship); introduction of specialized equipment requirements (oxygen tanks, explosives); increased officer/interrogation encounters
difficulty_curve.late_game: convergence at main base; prototype Metal Gear 2 threat; final confrontation with cybernetically enhanced Big Boss; teammate casualties (Nick killed, Jennifer captured then rescued)
notable_spikes: cargo ship explosion sequence and escape
underwater oxygen tank sections requiring resource management

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard stealth progression through facility layouts; guard detection and alert management; card key gating; item collection tied to guard defeats and environmental exploration
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Jungle base, warehouse, main base facility

notable_level_patterns.description: Horizontal corridor progression mimicking Rush 'n Attack; weapon limitation (knife/handgun only); jumping/crawling/stomach-crawling mechanics; underwater variants requiring oxygen management
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Cargo ship sequence, main base side-view passages

notable_level_patterns.description: Story-critical encounters that enforce combat engagement; John impostor mid-game; Big Boss as final climactic encounter with narrative revelations
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-base impostor fight, commander's lair final battle

notable_level_patterns.description: Equipment capacity and access locked behind star rank; hostage rescue and interrogation count toward promotions; creates resource management feedback loop
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Throughout game; rank 6 (max) unlocks highest equipment capacity
